message:
skill-loop pass 4/10: share env numeric parser

## Change: share integer env parsing helper

### Equivalence contract

- Inputs covered: env_u16, env_u32, env_u64, and env_usize callers.

- Ordering preserved: yes; each wrapper still performs one env_value lookup followed by parse.

- Tie-breaking: unchanged; source precedence stays inside env_value.

- Error semantics: unchanged; parse failure still falls back to the provided default.

- Laziness: unchanged.

- Short-circuit eval: unchanged.

- Floating-point: N/A; env_f64 intentionally untouched.

- RNG / hash order: unchanged.

- Observable side-effects: unchanged; optional parsers and f64 finite filtering are untouched.

### Verification

- cargo fmt --check

- git diff --check

- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core config::tests:: --locked

- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets

- cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets -- -D warnings

- ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs ran; env-var name findings were false positives

files.patch:
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 # Total Passes: 10
 # Started: 2026-04-25T01:08:13Z
 
-## Status: IN PROGRESS - Pass 4 of 10
+## Status: IN PROGRESS - Pass 5 of 10
 
 ## Architecture Summary
 - Workspace is a Rust 2024 Cargo workspace with strict layering: `core` owns config/models/errors/metrics; `db` owns SQLite schema/query/cache/search persistence; `storage` owns Git archive and commit coalescing; `tools` owns the MCP tool implementations; `server` owns stdio/HTTP dispatch, TUI/web/ATC/runtime workers; `cli` owns `am`, robot mode, operator commands, gates, E2E, and benchmarks.
@@ -48,3 +48,11 @@
 - Verification: worker fresh-eyes inspection passed; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools search::tests:: --locked`; `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets`; `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs` ran and reported one false-positive critical on local variable `token` in `parse_importance_list`, while its fmt/clippy/check/test subchecks were clean.
 - Commit: `058b129b`.
 - Verdict: PRODUCTIVE.
+
+### Pass 4 - Core Env Numeric Parser Simplification - 2026-04-25T02:19:00Z
+- Files changed: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs`.
+- Changes: added private generic `env_parse<T: std::str::FromStr>` and delegated `env_u16`, `env_u32`, `env_u64`, and `env_usize` to it.
+- Preservation notes: the shared helper uses the exact previous parse/fallback body; integer parsing still does not trim, defaults are unchanged, env source precedence is unchanged, `env_f64` finite filtering is untouched, and optional parsers keep their trimmed-empty semantics.
+- Verification: worker fresh-eyes inspection passed; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core config::tests:: --locked`; `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets`; `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs` ran and reported false-positive criticals on environment-variable names such as `HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN`/`HTTP_JWT_SECRET`, while its fmt/clippy/check/test subchecks were clean.
+- Commit: pending.
+- Verdict: PRODUCTIVE.

@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+# Pass 4 - Core Env Numeric Parser Simplification
+
+## Change
+
+The integer environment helpers now share a private generic `env_parse` helper.
+The public/local typed wrappers remain in place, so call sites and type
+inference behavior are unchanged.
+
+## Isomorphism Card
+
+- Inputs covered: `env_u16`, `env_u32`, `env_u64`, and `env_usize` callers.
+- Ordering preserved: yes; each wrapper still performs one `env_value` lookup
+  and then parses that value.
+- Error semantics: unchanged; parse failures still fall back to the provided
+  default via `.ok().unwrap_or(default)`.
+- Whitespace behavior: unchanged; integer values are still parsed without
+  trimming.
+- Observable side effects: unchanged; the same `env_value` source precedence is
+  used.
+- Exclusions: `env_f64` and optional integer parsers were intentionally left
+  alone because they have different finite-filtering and empty-string behavior.
+
+## Verification
+
+- `cargo fmt --check` - passed.
+- `git diff --check` - passed.
+- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core config::tests:: --locked` - passed, 178 tests.
+- `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets` - passed.
+- `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` - passed.
+- `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs` - ran; reported false-positive
+  criticals on environment-variable names such as `HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN` and
+  `HTTP_JWT_SECRET`, with fmt/clippy/check/test subchecks clean.
